message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My task: add domain= parameter support to trans tag (InternationalizationExtension.parse/_make_node/_install_callables) in src/jinja2/ext.py. Not sure what your feature touches - please share your files so we can split ext.py changes safely, e.g. by function or with sequential patches.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: I need to modify InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py to add trans_metadata support (note/created/modified params). If your feature also touches ext.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections or I can merge your changes in too.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both our features (domain= param vs trans_metadata note/created/modified params) touch parse()/_make_node()/_install_callables() in the same file, so a clean file-level split isn't possible. Let's both edit src/jinja2/ext.py but keep our additions in separate, clearly-delimited code blocks (my domain parsing logic vs your metadata parsing logic) so they interleave without semantic conflict. I'll re-Read the file immediately before each Edit to avoid clobbering your concurrent changes, and will only touch lines needed for domain support. Ping me with DONE when your edits land so I can do a final compatibility pass before writing patch.txt (git diff, so it will naturally include only actual changes present at that time).

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Our features both touch the same methods (parse() param loop, _make_node signature) so a disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: sequential ownership - I'll implement my trans_metadata feature first (small, additive: new metadata dict parsed in the param loop, new optional metadata param on _make_node, sets output_node.trans_metadata only when non-empty). I'll keep my diff minimal and clearly separated so it's easy to see. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact lines changed once finished, then you take over ext.py for domain= support and build on top of my changes. Sound good? If you'd rather go first, let me know and I'll wait instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - sequential ownership. You go first with trans_metadata, send DONE with summary of exact lines/changes when finished, then I'll build domain= support on top. I'll wait until I hear DONE from you before editing ext.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed. I'm starting now on the metadata feature (parse loop + _make_node). Will keep changes minimal/additive and send DONE shortly so you can layer domain= support on top.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Metadata feature complete in src/jinja2/ext.py. Summary of changes: added class attr _metadata_param_names = ('note','created','modified') on InternationalizationExtension; in parse(), added a 'metadata: t.Dict[str,str] = {}' dict, changed the comma-check 'if variables:' to 'if variables or metadata:' (so commas are still required correctly between params when a metadata param was the previous token), changed duplicate-name check to also look in metadata, and added a branch before the existing 'expressions' branch that intercepts note/created/modified: requires '=' then a parsed expression that must be nodes.Const with str value (else TemplateAssertionError with the two specified messages), stores into metadata dict, then 'continue'. metadata is now passed as a new 7th positional arg to self._make_node(...) (after num_called_num). _make_node() signature gained 'metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str,str]] = None' as its last param (default None so any other caller/signature you add is unaffected as long as you also pass positionally-consistent args or use keyword). At the end of _make_node, changed 'return nodes.Output([node])' to build 'output_node = nodes.Output([node])', then 'if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata', then return output_node. All 830 existing tests still pass. If your domain= work also needs to add params to parse()/_make_node(), please add your new metadata-loop branch as its own separate block (like mine) and add your own new _make_node parameter with a default value so ordering/back-compat stays safe - happy to help resolve any merge friction. My patch.txt is written from 'git diff' at repo root; let me know if you need me to hold off before you write yours so git diff captures both changes together, or if you're generating patches independently.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain feature complete. Note: in my sandbox, git diff was clean before I started (your metadata changes weren't present in my copy of the repo), so it looks like we're each working in isolated copies rather than a truly shared filesystem - no clobbering occurred. Summary of my changes to src/jinja2/ext.py: added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext (pass_context wrappers mirroring _make_new_pgettext); in parse(), added 'domain: Optional[str] = None' and a dedicated branch (checked before the generic assign branch) that intercepts 'domain=' followed by an expression, requiring it to be a string-literal nodes.Const (else TemplateAssertionError mentioning 'string literal constant'), and rejecting a second domain= (TemplateAssertionError mentioning 'specified twice'); changed the comma-guard to 'if variables or domain is not None:'; domain is passed as a new 7th positional arg to _make_node (default None). In _make_node, singular/plural Call nodes now target dgettext/dngettext with domain prepended as first arg when domain is set, else the existing gettext/ngettext path is unchanged. In _install_callables, added optional dgettext/dngettext params with a fallback that ignores domain and delegates to plain gettext/ngettext when translations don't provide them, wrapped for newstyle same as pgettext/npgettext; _install() now sources dgettext/dngettext off the translations object like pgettext/npgettext; _uninstall() also pops the two new globals. Also added dgettext/dngettext to GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS for babel extraction. Full suite: 830 passed. Wrote patch.txt via 'git diff' at repo root, containing only this feature.
